hero.name: Brahma Vihara Arama
hero.eyebrow: Banjar Tegeha · north Bali · Buleleng regency · ~11 km from Lovina · the island's largest Buddhist monastery
hero.background: radial-gradient(ellipse at 35% 25%, hsl(45 65% 60% / 0.55) 0%, transparent 55%), radial-gradient(ellipse at 80% 80%, hsl(20 35% 22% / 0.7) 0%, transparent 60%), linear-gradient(180deg, hsl(40 55% 60%) 0%, hsl(25 40% 35%) 50%, hsl(20 30% 18%) 100%)
hero.welcome_md:
The largest Buddhist monastery on Bali, in the village of Banjar Tegeha (Banjar sub-district, Buleleng regency), about 22 km west of Singaraja and 11 km from the tourist area of Lovina. The monastery is also known as the *Banjar Buddhist Temple*. Holy hot springs nearby; hiking trails through the surrounding terrain.

*Two to three times a year, the temple invites kirtan leader [Vasudev Baba](/people/vasudev-baba) and Prof Jem Bendell to gather friends for a long weekend of meditative silence, spiritual singing, nature hiking, soaking at the holy hot springs, and a collective satsang. The retreats have been continuous since 2020 and have become a nourishing rhythm for locals and long-resident expats.*

facts.label: Where
facts.value_md: Banjar Tegeha, Banjar sub-district, Buleleng regency, north Bali. ~22 km west of Singaraja; ~11 km from Lovina.

facts.label: Format of the retreats
facts.value_md: Long weekend (Friday to Sunday/Monday). Meditative silence woven with kirtan, nature hiking, holy hot springs nearby, collective satsang. Sometimes Dances of Universal Peace.

facts.label: Frequency
facts.value_md: 2–3 times a year. Continuous since 2020. The next confirmed editions are May and August 2026 (verify via the WhatsApp announcement group through Prof Bendell).

facts.label: Welcoming
facts.value_md: The temple welcomes locals and long-resident expats; it discourages international travelers from flying in specifically for the retreats. Donations to the temple are welcomed.

facts.label: Public anchors
facts.value_md: [Brahmavihara-Arama (Wikipedia)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brahmavihara-Arama) [Bali Temple Retreats — Prof Jem Bendell](https://jembendell.com/bali-temple-retreats/) [Bali Holiday Secrets feature](https://www.baliholidaysecrets.com/brahmavihara-arama/)

articles.body_md: Few bhakti-lineage singers are invited into Buddhist meditation hold; the temple keeps inviting Vasudev Baba back. The format weaves the two streams in real time — meditative silence as the substrate, kirtan as the singing voice that arrives inside that silence rather than displacing it. Nature hiking and the holy hot springs are the body-care that supports both. The collective satsang at the close lets the practice settle into shared speech. The retreats have become a nourishing, rebalancing rhythm in the year for locals and long-resident expats; the temple discourages international travelers from flying in for them.
articles.heading: Bhakti voice in Buddhist silence

articles.body_md:
- The room where bhakti voice and Buddhist silence cross — a rare hold in the contemporary spiritual-retreat field. Pairs with [lc-frequency-routes-reception](/vision/lc-frequency-routes-reception) (two streams in the same room, each tuned in its own band).
- The four-element retreat format (silence + song + walking + hot-spring + satsang) → [lc-each-breath-whole](/vision/lc-each-breath-whole) and [lc-ground-harder-when-field-quickens](/vision/lc-ground-harder-when-field-quickens) (the body practices grounding through earth, water, voice, silence in one container).
- The temple's discouragement of international travel for the retreats → the body's reading of [lc-voice-over-intentions](/vision/lc-voice-over-intentions) (respect the room's own voice; do not impose a larger frame).
articles.heading: What Brahma Vihara Arama has given the Coherence Network

metadata.title: Brahma Vihara Arama — the largest Buddhist monastery on Bali · long-weekend silent retreats | Coherence Network
metadata.description: A welcome to Brahma Vihara Arama in Banjar Tegeha, north Bali — the largest Buddhist monastery on the island. Two to three times a year hosts the long-weekend silent retreats co-held by Vasudev Baba and Prof Jem Bendell since 2020.

note_from_body.body_md: The monastery is a working Buddhist temple with its own monastic tending; this page recognises specifically the long-weekend retreats hosted there and their role in this body's Ubud lineage.
